commands.repair_reason: - [P2] No repair lane is needed; this PR is already the focused candidate fix and has no concrete review finding for automation to address.; Cleared: The diff only changes local backup JSONL parsing/serialization and tests; it does not touch dependencies, workflows, secrets, permissions, or downloaded code. (sha=b30b21808619e24d1deff9136387c4527c775f79)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates backup JSONL export/import to escape U+2028/U+2029, split import records on physical LF records, and adds round-trip backup coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main uses node:readline for backup JSONL and a Node probe showed createInterface splitting one physical JSON row at U+2028. I did not run a full backup import because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed; this PR is already the focused candidate fix and has no concrete review finding for automation to address.

target.title: fix: preserve JSONL rows with unicode separators
target.body:
## Summary

- escape literal `U+2028` / `U+2029` when writing JSONL backup values
- read backup JSONL using physical `\n` records instead of Node `readline` logical lines
- cover round-trip export/import with Unicode line and paragraph separators in collection raw JSON

Fixes #61.

## Checks

- `pnpm test src/lib/backup.test.ts`
- `pnpm run format:check`
- `pnpm run lint`
- `pnpm run typecheck`
- `pnpm test`

## Proof

```
❯ cd ~/dev/forks/birdclaw
git switch fix/jsonl-line-separators
git pull --ff-only
echo "branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
echo "commit: $(git rev-parse --short HEAD)"
pnpm exec tsx src/cli.ts backup sync --repo ~/Documents/Backup/X
```
